name: mxcheck
description: mxcheck is an info and security scanner for e-mail servers.
readme_summary: mxcheck is a Go-based scanner for e-mail servers, providing comprehensive checks on DNS records, security protocols, open ports, blacklist status, and potential vulnerabilities like open relays and SMTPSmuggling. It allows for customizable sender/receiver addresses and outputs reports in TSV format.
key_features: DNS record checks (A, MX, PTR, SPF, MTA-STS, DKIM, DMARC)
Information leakage detection (server string, VRFY command)
Customizable mailFrom, mailTo, and DNS server
primary_use_case: Scanning and assessing the security and configuration of email servers.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant digital detective, holding a magnifying glass that glows with network data, is meticulously examining a stylized email server represented as a complex, multi-layered castle. The magnifying glass highlights various aspects of the castle: a banner showing DNS records, a portcullis indicating open ports, and a flag revealing blacklist status. Subtle digital particles representing security checks flow from the detective towards the castle, with some parts of the castle glowing red (indicating issues) and others green (indicating good standing). The repository name 'mxcheck' is subtly integrated into the detective's badge. The scene is set in a dark, futuristic cityscape with a cool, blue-toned lighting, emphasizing a cybersecurity theme. Rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with clean lines and a focus on clear visual metaphors.

name: frame
readme_summary: Frame is a high-performance media conversion utility that provides a native graphical interface for FFmpeg operations. It allows users to control transcoding parameters, leverage hardware acceleration, and even perform AI upscaling, all managed by a Rust backend and a Svelte frontend.
key_features: Support for various media containers (mp4, mkv, webm, etc.)
Multiple video and audio encoders (H.264, H.265, VP9, AV1, AAC, Opus, etc.)
Native GUI built with Tauri and Svelte
primary_use_case: A user-friendly desktop application for converting and transcoding video and audio files with advanced options and AI upscaling.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, magical anvil in a clean, modern workshop. Raw, unrefined video files (represented as rough metallic blocks) are being placed onto the anvil. A glowing hammer, labeled 'Frame', strikes the blocks, transforming them into polished, high-definition video reels that smoothly roll off the anvil. Subtle digital particles and small, clean UI elements showing codec options float around the scene. The background features a stylized, minimalist interface hinting at Svelte and Tauri. The overall mood is efficient and transformative. 3D isometric illustration style, vibrant yet clean color palette, high detail.

name: terminalizer
description: 🦄 Record your terminal and generate animated gif images or share a web player
readme_summary: Terminalizer is a command-line tool that records terminal sessions and generates animated GIF files or shareable web player links. It offers extensive customization for recording parameters, output styles, and terminal themes, making it ideal for creating demonstrations and tutorials.
key_features: Customizable window frames, fonts, and colors
Skip frames for reduced file size
Render images with text for better quality
primary_use_case: Recording terminal sessions to create animated GIFs or web player links for demonstrations, tutorials, or sharing command-line workflows.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical quill pen meticulously writing lines of code onto a parchment scroll. As the quill writes, the text on the scroll transforms into a vibrant, animated GIF of a bustling command-line interface, complete with colorful text and a subtle cursor blinking. Small, glowing data particles emanate from the quill, symbolizing the recording process. The background is a softly lit, cozy study with a hint of digital circuitry integrated into the bookshelves. The overall mood is creative and productive, showcasing the transformation of raw terminal input into a polished, shareable visual. 3D isometric illustration style with a clean, modern aesthetic.

name: gosuki
description: A lightweight, open-source, privacy-first bookmark manager that unifies your bookmarks across multiple browsers, syncs them in real time (locally or P2P), requires no extensions, and stores everything locally.
readme_summary: GoSuki is a privacy-focused, extension-free bookmark manager written in Go. It unifies bookmarks across multiple browsers and profiles, offering real-time local or P2P synchronization with a portable database and optional archiving capabilities.
primary_use_case: To provide a unified, private, and synchronized bookmark management solution across various browsers and devices without relying on cloud services or browser extensions.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian meticulously organizing a vast, interconnected library where each book represents a bookmark from a different browser (Chrome, Firefox, Brave, etc.). The librarian is using a magical, glowing quill that instantly duplicates and syncs bookmark entries across multiple floating shelves, each labeled with a different browser icon. Small, subtle digital streams of data flow between the shelves, representing P2P sync. The library itself is a cozy, private study, free from any external cloud symbols. The overall mood is one of calm control and organized knowledge. The art style should be a detailed digital painting with a warm, inviting color palette, emphasizing the 'extension-free' and 'privacy-first' aspects through the librarian's focused, independent work.

name: CodeMachine-CLI
description: CodeMachine is an open-source tool that orchestrates AI coding agents into repeatable, long-running workflows. ⚡️
readme_summary: CodeMachine-CLI is an open-source tool designed to orchestrate AI coding agents into repeatable, long-running workflows. It allows developers to define complex coding processes once and then execute them reliably, automating repetitive tasks and enabling multi-agent collaboration.
key_features: Orchestrates AI coding agents into workflows
Allows for parallel execution of agents
Handles workflow persistence for long-running tasks
Provides context engineering for agent prompts and data
primary_use_case: Automating and standardizing complex, multi-step coding tasks by orchestrating AI agents into defined workflows.
cover_image_prompt: A master conductor standing in front of an orchestra pit, not with musical instruments, but with various AI coding agents represented as distinct robotic figures. The conductor holds a glowing baton, directing the agents to perform specific coding tasks, with lines of light connecting the baton to each agent and showing data flow. Input code snippets are being fed into a central processing unit, and the output is a beautifully rendered, complex software architecture blueprint materializing on a large screen behind the orchestra. Subtle UI elements and code snippets are visible on small floating screens around the scene. The CodeMachine-CLI logo is subtly integrated into the conductor's podium. The scene is set in a futuristic, clean studio with dramatic lighting, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant, glowing accents.

name: alt-sendme
description: Send files and folders anywhere in the world without storing in cloud - any size, any format, no accounts, no restrictions.
readme_summary: AltSendme is a free, open-source, and privacy-focused tool for direct peer-to-peer file transfers. It enables users to send any file or folder of any size across the globe without relying on cloud storage, ensuring end-to-end encryption and no account requirements.
key_features: No accounts or personal info required
primary_use_case: Direct, secure, and unrestricted file and folder transfers between users without intermediaries or cloud storage.
cover_image_prompt: A conceptual illustration of two friendly, stylized robots on opposite sides of a vibrant, stylized globe. One robot is handing a glowing, transparent package directly to the other robot, bypassing any cloud servers depicted as small, distant, inactive satellites. The package is clearly labeled 'AltSendme' and emits subtle digital particles. The connection between the robots is a bright, direct beam of light, symbolizing a secure peer-to-peer connection. The background is a clean, modern digital space with subtle network lines. The overall mood is efficient, secure, and user-friendly. 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, clean color palette.
